questions.text: Which of these is a producer in an ecosystem?
questions.explanation: Grass is a producer (autotroph) that makes its own food through photosynthesis. Deer and lion are consumers, bacteria are decomposers.

questions.text: Ozone layer is present in the stratosphere.
questions.explanation: The ozone layer is located in the stratosphere, about 15-35 km above Earth's surface.

questions.text: What is the role of decomposers in an ecosystem?
questions.correctAnswer: They break down dead organic matter and recycle nutrients.
questions.explanation: Decomposers (bacteria, fungi) break down dead plants and animals, releasing nutrients back into the ecosystem for reuse by producers.

questions.text: The first trophic level in a food chain is always ________.
questions.explanation: Producers (green plants) form the first trophic level as they produce food through photosynthesis.
